channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Curious about DeepSeek but worried about privacy? These apps let you use an LLM without the internet
item.description: Most of us are used to using internet chatbots like ChatGPT and DeepSeek in one of two ways: via a web browser or via their dedicated smartphone apps. There are two drawbacks to this. First, their use requires an internet connection.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nvidia's RTX 5090 has already been pushed beyond its limit - ROG Astral model overclock reaches 3.45GHz and 35 Gbps VRAM
item.description: For once, there is some good news regarding the RTX 5000 series GPUs, and that's because the Asus ROG Astral RTX 5090 OC has just broken multiple overclocking world records.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Netflix in March 2025 – stream the miniseries Adolescence and new Original movies
item.description: Netflix's March 2025 list of new movies and shows is a reflection of why it's still one of the best streaming services. Here's everything new this month, including original shows and old favorites.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: What is an AI server? Why artificial intelligence needs specialized systems
item.description: AI servers are advanced computing systems designed to handle complex, resource-intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI Sora is now available in the UK and EU for AI video – here's how to use it
item.description: Sora is expanding outside the US, which means paying ChatGPT users in more countries can give it a try.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Do more with Python’s new built-in async programming library
item.description: There’s lots to do in this edition of the Python Report: Do more than one thing with Python’s async. Do the math faster in Python with NumPy. Do Python in Visual Studio Code, and do it the right way with our get-started guide.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: MasOrange acelera su transformación digital avivada por la IA mediante una alianza con Google Cloud
item.description: A las puertas de la celebración de una nueva edición del Mobile World Congress (MWC), MasOrange anuncia una nueva alianza estratégica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI unveils its new GPT-4.5 large language model
item.description: OpenAI released a new base model on Thursday called GPT-4.5, which the company said is its best and smartest model for chat yet. It’s not a reasoning model like OpenAI’s o1 and o3 models, but it can be used to train other models to be reasoning models.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: This new bill aims to make presidential meme coins illegal
item.description: California Democrat Rep. Sam Liccardo, a freshman congressman who represents Silicon Valley, said he’s surprised the first piece of legislation he’s sponsoring takes aim at President Donald Trump’s meme coin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 7 ways to fight back against spam phone calls
item.description: Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it’s a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it’s enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump promised to keep spying agencies in check. Then he fired the watchdogs he appointed
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to fight government abuse and introduce more transparency, a stance that might align him with a little-known agency charged with watching over the U.S.’s powerful spying programs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Phi-4-multimodal AI model handles speech, text, and video
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new AI model that, it says, can process speech, vision, and text locally on-device using less compute capacity than previous models.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meme coins aren’t just harmless fun
item.description: For some time, meme coins have occupied a peculiar space in online culture. While there are people who have struck it rich trading these joke-based cryptocurrencies, the landscape is riddled with scams, “rug pulls,” and market manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Android phones to drive mobile sales in 2025
item.description: Global sales of smartphones will increase by 2.3% in 2025 compared to last year, according to a new report from IDC. Android-based phones are expected to account for most of the increase, including in China, where sales are expected to rise by 5.6% year over year.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeepSeek offers steep discounts, escalating AI price war
item.description: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek has unveiled a significant price reduction for developers using its AI models, a move that could intensify competition among both domestic and global rivals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX prepares for 8th Starship launch, pending FAA approval
item.description: SpaceX is slated to launch its Starship for the eighth time from Boca Chica, Texas, Friday, though plans could be thwarted if the FAA fails to give the green light.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Las Vegas PD announce nation’s first fleet of police Tesla Cybertrucks
item.description: The Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department (LVMPD) announced it had been gifted the country’s first fleet of Tesla Cybertrucks for law enforcement use. During an address, LVMPD said an unnamed donor gifted the department at least 10 Cybertrucks to use for patrol and SWAT teams.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Tech Conferences 2025 Calendar
item.description: Upcoming network, I&O, and data center conferences in 2025
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, automation spur efforts to upskill network pros
item.description: Networking skills are evolving to keep pace with broader technology trends, and network pros need to proactively develop new competencies in areas such as AI and automation to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Reported cuts at NIST imperil semiconductor reshoring plans in the US
item.description: Proposed cuts at a key US government agency will likely mean the demise of CHIPS Act funding, which could effectively end the program and undermine efforts to reshore semiconductor manufacturing and research to the US.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM acquires DataStax to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities
item.description: IBM has announced its intent to acquire database and related services provider DataStax for an undisclosed sum in an effort to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM targets AI application growth with DataStax buy
item.description: Looking to bolster its AI data handling and storage capabilities, IBM said it had entered an agreement to buy open-source database developer DataStax for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Review: Gemini Code Assist is good at coding
item.description: Given that Microsoft/GitHub/Azure and Amazon/AWS offer AI coding assistants, namely GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer, which work as add-ins to Visual Studio Code and other IDEs, it was inevitable that Google/GCP would introduce one of its own.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies
item.description: VA officials are working on an updated EHR deployment schedule beyond the Michigan go-lives in mid-2026. The post VA EHR office shrinks headcount under Trump’s federal workforce policies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: This is what you are doing wrong when scammers call
item.description: The line between spam and scams is thin. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson details his tips for identifying and avoiding phone scams, robocalls and unwanted solicitations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
